signals.reasoning: BTC is stabilizing but still sitting inside a weak macro backdrop. The tape is not clean enough to justify a fresh directional call here.
ETH continues to behave like a muted post-flush reset rather than a clean breakout or breakdown. Neutral remains the disciplined call.
SOL still looks more like a stabilization bounce after liquidation than a fresh continuation setup. That keeps it off the actionable list.
0G still carries the strongest squeeze-long setup on the sheet. Funding remains negative while price and open interest continue to rise together, which keeps the trend-supported squeeze thesis alive.
AVNT is recovering from a prior liquidation flush and is more constructive than broken, but the rebound is still not strong enough to justify upgrading it beyond watch-only.
CHILLGUY is bouncing, but the move reads more like a short-covering rally than a clean durable trend turn. Interesting, but not strong enough to promote.
NIL remains too thin and too flat to offer a meaningful edge. No reason to force a trade.
DOOD has a real rebound going, but the execution quality is still too poor to trust for a fresh signal. Better to leave it alone.
ALT is stabilizing, but there is no real squeeze or breakdown edge here. The setup is too neutral to justify a strong directional call.
STABLE remains one of the few names with real squeeze fuel. Funding is still negative and predicted to stay negative while price holds strong, so it keeps a reduced-size long lean.
GOAT is still damaged, but the downside is already too mature and unstable to trust as a fresh short. Weak does not equal clean entry.
